AAPL.reasoning: Branch B fires weakly; Branch A does not fire. Branch A assessment: daily Z-score of 1.21 is within the -2 to +2 neutral band, RSI-14 at 60.63 and RSI-21 at 66.79 are elevated but below the >75 overbought threshold, Bollinger %B at 0.72 is in the upper half but not outside the bands, and price is only 3.64% above the 50-SMA (below the 5% noteworthy threshold). No statistical extreme exists and RSI divergence is bullish rather than bearish, so there is no fade setup. Branch B assessment: all EMAs are aligned in an uptrend, confirming a dominant upward structure. Price pulled back from the recent swing high of 275.77 to a low of 265.07 on April 27, testing near the 23.6% Fibonacci retracement at 268.63. That level held and price has since recovered to ~270-271. The short-term bounce is already underway, so the ideal entry window has partially passed. Concerns that limit confidence: (1) ADX is 15.78, still in weak-trend territory — the wiki explicitly flagged sub-15 ADX across multiple prior runs and the current read barely clears that bar; (2) volume was not clearly declining on the April 27 pullback (41.5M vs 40.8M 20-day avg), a mild warning; (3) the RSI-7 at 43.9 is approaching but not at oversold levels for a pullback confirmation; (4) wiki context notes repeated failed bounce attempts at similar chart setups since April 11-17, which lowers fresh-fade conviction. The 23.6% Fib is a shallow retracement level — a deeper pullback to the 38.2% Fib (~264.21) near the 20-SMA (264.36) would be a cleaner Branch B setup. The current entry is best treated as a partial/starter position with tight management. Target is the swing high retest at 275.77. Stop is below the 38.2% Fib / 20-SMA cluster at 263.50, which would signal the pullback has deepened beyond a healthy Branch B structure.
